knowYourself.name: Know Yourself
knowYourself.shortDesc: Figure out your skills, values, and brand before you start applying.
knowYourself.longDesc: Map your skills, write a brand statement, and build a 30-second pitch. Everything here feeds into your resume.

applications.longDesc: Keep one master form with all your info. Pull from it whenever an employer asks the same questions again.

resumes.longDesc: Build a resume that pulls from your skills inventory. Export as PDF in one click.

interviews.longDesc: Write STAR stories, practice answering questions, and get feedback before the real thing.

landing.heroTitle: Land your first job, step by step.
landing.heroSubtitle: 8 connected tools that walk you from figuring out your strengths to nailing the interview. Built for new grads. Write once, auto-fill everywhere.
landing.trustBadge: No sign-up. No paywall. Just open a tool and start typing.

benefits.privateData: Keep your data private in your browser
benefits.autoFill: Auto-fill your resume from your skills inventory
benefits.exportPdf: Export to PDF in one click

landing.phaseSectionTitle: From "what am I good at?" to signed offer.
landing.phaseSectionSubtitle: Three phases, eight tools. Go in order or jump to what you need. What you write in one tool shows up in the others.

phase1.tagline: Figure out what you're actually good at.
phase1.desc: Before you apply anywhere, get clear on what you bring. Map your skills, rank your work values, and write a 30-second pitch you can use at networking events.

phase2.tagline: Build your resume. Prep your answers.
phase2.desc: Your brand statement auto-fills into your resume, cover letter, and interview prep. Practice STAR stories, search job boards, and apply with materials that actually match the posting.

phase3.tagline: Track applications. Crush your first 90 days.
phase3.desc: Log every application you send, set follow-up reminders so nothing falls through, and get a self-evaluation checklist for your first months on the job.

landing.toolsGridSubtitle: We recommend going in order, but you can jump to whatever you need right now. Click any card to begin.
landing.quoteText: You write your skills and brand statement once in Know Yourself. After that, they auto-fill your resume, cover letter, and interview prep. No copy-pasting.
landing.quoteAttribution: That's the whole idea: write once, use everywhere.
landing.ctaDesc: Open Know Yourself, pick your top skills, and write a quick brand statement. Everything else builds from there.

footer.tagline: Job prep tools for new grads. Prep at your own pace. Data stays in your browser.
footer.bottomLine: Free and open to everyone. Your data stays in your browser.

knowYourself.title: Know Yourself
knowYourself.description: This is the foundation of your entire job search. What you write here auto-fills your resume, cover letter, and interview prep. You only write it once.
knowYourself.nextStepText: Once you've identified your skills and brand statement, head to
knowYourself.nextStepSuffix: where your data auto-fills. Your skills and brand statement also flow into cover letters and interview prep.

beliefs.title: Challenge Your Beliefs
beliefs.description: Replace negative thoughts with positive ones that help your job search.

focusGoals.title: Goal Setting with FOCUS
focusGoals.description: Set clear job search goals with the FOCUS method.

skills.title: Transferable Skills
skills.description: Pick your top skills. They auto-fill your resume.

workValues.title: Work Values
workValues.description: Rank what matters most to you in a workplace.

branding.title: Personal Branding
branding.description: Write your brand statement. It becomes your resume summary.

powerStatement.title: Power Statement
powerStatement.description: Write your 30-second pitch for networking events and interviews.

skills.title: Transferable Skills Inventory
skills.description: Identify the soft and hard skills you bring from all your experiences: jobs, internships, volunteer work, campus activities, and coursework.
skills.callout: Skills you pick here show up in your resume and interview prep automatically.
skills.softSkillsDesc: People skills and personal qualities. Select all that apply. Aim for at least 5.
skills.hardSkillsDesc: Specific, teachable abilities. Think about skills from coursework, certifications, projects, and jobs.

categories.technologyEx: Microsoft Office, Google Suite, programming, data analysis
categories.healthcareEx: CPR, patient care, medical records, lab procedures
categories.creativeEx: Graphic design, video editing, photography, writing
categories.otherEx: Forklift certified, bilingual, CDL, food handling

beliefs.title: Challenge Your Belief System
beliefs.description: Name what you believe about yourself, then turn challenges into positive statements.
beliefs.callout: "I don't have enough experience" — thoughts like this kill interviews. This exercise flips them so you can talk about yourself with confidence.
beliefs.iAmPlaceholder: e.g., "outgoing and get along well with others"
beliefs.iCanPlaceholder: e.g., "communicate with people from various backgrounds"
beliefs.iHavePlaceholder: e.g., "the determination to do a good job"
beliefs.challengesDesc: Think of any negative beliefs about employment and convert them to positive affirmations.
beliefs.challengePlaceholder: I have had a challenge with...
beliefs.solutionPlaceholder: But I can overcome it by...

branding.title: Personal Branding
branding.description: Write 1-2 sentences that tell employers what you're good at and why.
branding.callout: This goes on your resume, LinkedIn, and anywhere you introduce yourself. Keep it to 1-2 sentences: what you do best, for whom, and what makes you different.
branding.skillsPlaceholder: What skills, qualities, and attributes should your brand convey?
branding.accomplishmentsPlaceholder: What are your accomplishments related to your brand?
branding.statementDesc: Write 1-2 sentences about what you are best at (value), who you serve (audience), and how you do it uniquely.
branding.statementPlaceholder: e.g., "Marketing graduate who ran social media for two campus organizations. I grew one account from 200 to 2,000 followers in a semester."
branding.example1: "I helped customers find the right product at a busy retail store and consistently hit 110% of my weekly sales target."
branding.example2: "CS graduate who built three full-stack apps in school. I care about making websites that work for everyone, including people using screen readers."

workValues.title: Work Values Assessment
workValues.description: Rank what matters most to you in a workplace. This helps you target jobs that align with your priorities.
workValues.callout: Use the arrows to reorder. #1 is most important to you. Your values may change over time. Revisit this periodically.

values.achievementDesc: Jobs that let you use your best abilities with a feeling of accomplishment.
values.relationshipsDesc: Jobs where co-workers are friendly and you can be of service to others.
values.supportDesc: Jobs with competent and fair management that stands behind its workers.
values.independenceDesc: Jobs where you are encouraged to take initiative and make decisions.
values.recognitionDesc: Jobs with good possibilities for advancement, prestige, and leadership.
values.workingConditionsDesc: Jobs with desired pay, security, location, and work style.

focusGoals.title: Goal Setting with FOCUS
focusGoals.description: Set your job search goals using the FOCUS method.
focusGoals.callout: If your goal is just a dream, take specific action steps to make it happen. The FOCUS framework breaks your goal into achievable parts.
focusGoals.findDesc: What exactly do you want to achieve? Be specific.
focusGoals.findPlaceholder: What exactly do you want to achieve? Be specific.
focusGoals.outlineDesc: Break your goal into smaller, achievable steps.
focusGoals.resourcesDesc: Who and what can help you achieve this goal?
focusGoals.purposeDesc: Why is this goal important to you?
focusGoals.purposePlaceholder: Why is this goal important to you?
focusGoals.timeframeDesc: When will you achieve this goal?
focusGoals.goalStatementPlaceholder: Combine all FOCUS elements into one complete goal statement...

powerStatement.title: Power Statement
powerStatement.description: Write a 30-second introduction you can use at networking events and in interviews.
powerStatement.callout: Your Power Statement can be used when networking, at job fairs, in interviews when asked "Tell me about yourself," and as the summary statement on your resume.
powerStatement.tip1: Be brief and conversational, but not too wordy
powerStatement.tip2: Emphasize your brand and show enthusiasm
powerStatement.tip3: Speak in results, not cliches like "I am a team player"
powerStatement.tip4: Focus on the benefits you bring to the employer
powerStatement.exampleText: "My name is Sarah Chen and I recently graduated with a degree in Marketing from UCLA. I specialize in social media strategy and content creation, with internship experience managing campaigns that increased engagement by 40%. I'm looking for an entry-level marketing coordinator role where I can bring fresh ideas and data-driven creativity to your team."
powerStatement.placeholder: Hi, I'm [name]. I just graduated from [school] with a degree in [field]. At [internship/job], I [specific thing you did]. I'm looking for a [target role] where I can...

applications.title: Applications
applications.description: Fill out applications faster. Stop re-typing the same information.

tips.title: 10 Essential Application Tips
tips.description: 10 tips to get your application past the first screening.

experienceGap.title: Experience Gap Solver
experienceGap.description: Turn volunteer work, freelance gigs, and internships into application material.

masterBuilder.title: Master Application Builder
masterBuilder.description: Keep all your info in one place so you can fill any application fast.

tips.title: 10 Essential Application Tips
tips.description: Follow these tips to complete effective applications that get you noticed.
tips.callout: Many applications are rejected because of errors or failure to tailor the content. Following up within 3–5 days shows your commitment and keeps your name on the employer's radar.
tips.tip1Desc: Make sure you meet most of the minimum qualifications: experience, education/certificates, and hard and soft skills.
tips.tip2Desc: Work history, education, references: create a master application with complete and accurate employment information to have on hand.
tips.tip3Desc: Read all directions before completing each section. Use the exact date format requested. List employment history in the order specified.
tips.tip4Desc: List all paid and unpaid work: volunteer, charity, community activities, internships, self-employment, and temporary experience. Don't leave gaps in your history.
tips.tip5Title: Use keywords from the job posting
tips.tip5Desc: Include transferable skills and keywords in the availability, education, and work history sections. Online applications use software to filter by keywords.
tips.tip6Title: Never leave the reference section blank
tips.tip6Desc: Always enter the number of references requested. Ask permission first, and verify current contact information for each reference.
tips.tip7Desc: Use proper capitalization and grammar. Use a dictionary, spell-checker, or have someone proofread before submitting.
tips.tip8Desc: For each application, submit a customized resume and cover letter that match the specified requirements.
tips.tip9Desc: Thoroughly review the application and all supporting documents before submitting. Check for errors, missing information, and consistency.
tips.tip10Title: Keep a copy for your records
tips.tip10Desc: Save or screenshot every application along with the job announcement. This helps when preparing for interviews and following up.

experienceGap.title: Experience Gap Solver
experienceGap.description: Think you don't have enough experience? You likely have more than you realize. Check all types of experience that apply to you.
experienceGap.callout: Employers count all types of experience. Volunteer work, campus activities, internships, and class projects all show skills that belong on your resume.
experienceGap.resultDesc: Add these to your resume and applications. Use the Resume Builder to turn each experience into result-oriented bullet points.

volunteer.title: Unpaid or Volunteer Work
volunteer.description: Have you done any work for family, friends, or organizations?
items: Community Service
Tutoring
Mentoring
Campus Organizations
Church/Faith-Based
School Events
Family Caregiving

internships.title: Internships & Training
internships.description: Did you attend college, trade school, or complete any training?
items: Internship
Externship
Certificate Program
On-the-Job Training
Job Shadowing
Research Assistant
Teaching Assistant

campus.title: Campus & Extracurricular
campus.description: Activities that show leadership and teamwork.
items: Student Government
Club Officer/Member
Sports Team
Student Newspaper
Event Planning
Peer Counseling
Resident Advisor

selfEmployment.title: Self-Employment & Freelance
selfEmployment.description: Did you work independently or from home?
items: Freelance Projects
Tutoring
Social Media Management
Web Design
Photography
Crafts/Etsy
Gig Work (DoorDash, etc.)

partTime.title: Part-Time & Temporary
partTime.description: Short-term or part-time work of any kind.
items: Seasonal Work
Retail
Food Service
Staffing Agency
Contract Work
Campus Job (Work-Study)
Summer Jobs

masterBuilder.title: Master Application Builder
masterBuilder.description: Keep all your application information in one place. Reference this when filling out any employer's application.
masterBuilder.callout: A master application saves time and keeps your info consistent. Include ALL experience: paid, unpaid, volunteer, and temporary.
masterBuilder.skillsPlaceholder: List your computer skills, software proficiency, and other abilities...
masterBuilder.certsPlaceholder: List any certificates, licenses, or training...

resumes.title: Resumes
resumes.description: Build your resume, cover letter, and reference page, then export as PDF.

builder.title: Resume Builder
builder.description: Fill in each section and see a live preview as you type.

coverLetter.title: Cover Letter Builder
coverLetter.description: Write a cover letter that matches each job you apply for.

references.title: Reference Page
references.description: Build a formatted reference page matching your resume.

emailGuide.title: Email Guide
emailGuide.description: Templates for emailing your resume to employers.

builder.title: Resume Builder
builder.description: Fill in each section and see your resume update in real time.
builder.autoFillPrompt: Pull in the skills and brand statement you wrote in Know Yourself?
builder.noDataCallout: Head to Know Yourself first and fill out your skills and brand statement. Once you do, they'll auto-fill here.
builder.profileDesc: A branding statement, summary, or list of qualifications. This is the top third of your resume.
builder.profilePlaceholder: e.g., Detail-oriented computer science graduate skilled in full-stack development...
builder.educationDesc: Add your degrees, certificates, and training.
builder.needOneResume: You need at least one resume.
builder.noExperience: No experience added yet. Include jobs, internships, volunteer work, and campus roles. Not sure what counts? Try the Experience Gap Solver in Applications.
builder.bulletPlaceholder: e.g., Increased customer satisfaction by 20% through...

coverLetter.title: Cover Letter Builder
coverLetter.description: Write a cover letter in three paragraphs: why you're interested, what you bring, and next steps.
coverLetter.callout: A cover letter isn't a repeat of your resume. Pick 1-2 experiences that match what the job posting asks for, explain how they connect, and say why this company specifically interests you.
coverLetter.openingDesc: State the position, how you heard about it, your top qualifications, and why you're interested.
coverLetter.openingPlaceholder: I'm applying for the [Position] I saw on [Source]. Here's why I'd be a good fit...
coverLetter.bodyDesc: Explain how your experience is relevant to the position. Mention unique skills or qualifications.
coverLetter.bodyPlaceholder: As a [your role/background], I gained valuable experience in...
coverLetter.closingDesc: Reiterate interest, mention follow-up timeline, and thank them.
coverLetter.closingPlaceholder: I'd welcome the chance to talk more about how my background fits this role. I'm available anytime next week...
coverLetter.saveFailed: Failed to save cover letter. Please try again.

aiCoverLetter.title: Generate with AI
aiCoverLetter.description: Paste a job description and your resume. The AI writes a first draft that maps your experience to the job. Edit it before saving.
aiCoverLetter.jdPlaceholder: Paste the full job posting here...
aiCoverLetter.notReady: Add a job description and resume to continue
aiCoverLetter.replaceConfirm: This will replace your current cover letter content. Continue?
aiCoverLetter.successMessage: Cover letter generated! Edit it below to make it yours.
aiCoverLetter.failedGeneric: Cover letter generation failed. Please try again.

references.title: Reference Page
references.description: Build a formatted reference page that matches your resume layout.
references.callout: List at least 5 people and double-check their phone numbers and emails. Ask permission first. Supervisors, professors, and co-workers make the strongest references.

emailGuide.title: Emailing Your Resume
emailGuide.description: Follow these guidelines when submitting your resume via email.
emailGuide.subjectDesc: Include the job title or position you are applying for and your full name.
emailGuide.subjectExample: Subject: Marketing Coordinator Position - Jane Smith
emailGuide.emailBodyDesc: Write a brief introductory message. You can use your cover letter as the body, or compose a shorter message and attach the cover letter.
emailGuide.emailTip1: Address to the contact person if possible
emailGuide.emailTip3: Reference 1-2 skills from the job posting that match your experience
emailGuide.contactInfoDesc: Include your full name, phone number, and email at the end of your email. Make it easy for the employer to contact you.
emailGuide.attachmentsDesc: Attach your resume and cover letter as PDF files. Name them clearly:
emailGuide.proofreadWarning: Double check your spelling, grammar, and capitalization before sending. Proofread everything. Emails create a first impression too.
emailGuide.sampleSubject: Subject: Marketing Coordinator Position - Jane Smith
emailGuide.sampleBody: I've attached my resume and cover letter for the Marketing Coordinator position. I recently graduated with a degree in Marketing and have internship experience managing social media campaigns. I'd love to bring that hands-on experience to your team.
emailGuide.sampleClosing: Thank you for your time. I look forward to hearing from you.

error.title: Resume tool error
error.description: Something went wrong loading your resumes. Your saved data is safe.

interviews.title: Interviews
interviews.description: Prep your stories, practice answers, research companies, and write thank you notes.

storybank.title: Storybank
storybank.description: Write STAR stories and tag them by skill so they're ready when you need them.

jdDecoder.title: JD Decoder
jdDecoder.description: Paste any job posting to see which stories you have ready and where you have gaps.

mockInterview.title: AI Mock Interview
mockInterview.description: Answer questions one by one and get AI coaching after each.

commonQuestions.title: Common Questions
commonQuestions.description: Draft answers to the 8 questions interviewers ask most.

companyResearch.title: Company Research
companyResearch.description: Organize your research on each company before you walk in.

thankYou.title: Thank You Notes
thankYou.description: Write and send thank you notes the same day.

starMethod.title: Storybank
starMethod.description: Write and polish your behavioral interview stories. Tag skills and track which questions you can answer.
starMethod.deleteStoryConfirm: This story will be permanently deleted.
starMethod.summary: You have {count} {storyWord} ({complete} complete). Aim for 6-8 stories with all four STAR fields filled in.

sampleQuestions.q1: Describe a difficult problem you solved.
sampleQuestions.q2: Describe a time when you disagreed with a supervisor.
sampleQuestions.q3: How do you deal with a difficult customer or client?
sampleQuestions.q4: Give an example of a time when you showed initiative.
sampleQuestions.q5: Tell me about a time you worked effectively under pressure.
sampleQuestions.q6: Describe a situation where you had to learn something new quickly.

commonQuestions.title: Common Interview Questions
commonQuestions.description: Prepare answers to the 8 most common interview questions.
commonQuestions.generalTips: Hit these 4 points in every answer: (1) Connect your experience to the job, (2) Use their language with real examples, (3) Sound confident, not rehearsed, (4) Stay focused — don't ramble.
commonQuestions.powerStatementPrompt: Write your Power Statement first. It's your answer to 'Tell me about yourself.'

q1.question: Tell me about yourself.
tips: Keep your response related to the job you are seeking.
Use your power statement as a starting point.
Highlight education, relevant experience, and key skills.

q2.question: Why are you the best person for this job?
tips: Match your qualifications to the job description.
Describe specific skills and experience you bring.
Back up each claim with a specific example.

q3.question: Why do you want to work here?
tips: Include information learned through researching the company.
Convey enthusiasm about the company and the role.
Show you understand the company's mission and values.

q4.question: What are your strengths?
tips: Identify 3 skills that qualify you for the job.
Be genuine. Choose strengths you can back up with examples.
Provide specific examples for each strength.

q5.question: What are your weaknesses?
tips: Choose a skill you've actively worked to improve.
Describe the steps you've taken to improve.
Don't mention weaknesses that would disqualify you.

q6.question: How would co-workers describe you?
tips: Share personality traits that match the job and culture.
Talk about how you work with others day to day.

q7.question: Why did you leave your last job?
tips: Be honest, but don't criticize your last employer.
Stay positive and focus on what you learned.
Frame it as seeking growth or new opportunities.

q8.question: Where do you see yourself in five years?
tips: Mention career goals that align with the company.
State that you see yourself advancing within the company.
Show ambition while being realistic.

companyResearch.title: Company Research
companyResearch.description: Research each company before your interview. Use this framework to organize your findings.
companyResearch.callout: What to research: How long they've been in business, products/services, mission statement, locations, recent news, the role's purpose, growth opportunities, and salary/benefits.
companyResearch.newsPlaceholder: Any recent announcements, awards, or changes?

thankYou.title: Thank You Note Generator
thankYou.description: Send a thank you note within 24 hours of your interview.
thankYou.callout: A quick thank you note makes you stand out. Most candidates skip this step. Send it within 24 hours while the conversation is still fresh.
thankYou.keyPoints: Key Discussion Points / Why You're Excited
thankYou.keyPointsPlaceholder: Mention specific things discussed, what excited you about the role...
thankYou.templatePara1: Thank you for meeting with me today. It was a pleasure to learn more about the {position} role and the team at {company}.
thankYou.templatePara2: Our conversation confirmed that this role is a great match for what I do best. I'm looking forward to the possibility of joining the team.
thankYou.templatePara3: Feel free to reach out if you need anything else from me. I hope to hear from you soon.

jdDecoder.title: JD Decoder
jdDecoder.description: Paste a job posting. The AI pulls out what they want, checks which of your stories fit, and flags what's missing.
jdDecoder.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again.
jdDecoder.inputPlaceholder: Paste the full job posting here...
jdDecoder.deleteAnalysisConfirm: This analysis will be permanently deleted.

mockInterview.title: AI Mock Interview
mockInterview.description: Pick a question set, answer each one, and get feedback before moving on.
mockInterview.quickPracticeDesc: 6 common questions that come up in almost every interview.
mockInterview.specificJobDesc: Questions pulled from a job posting you already analyzed.
mockInterview.answerPlaceholder: Answer as if you're talking to the interviewer right now.
mockInterview.summaryUnavailable: Summary not available for this session.
mockInterview.noSessions: No practice sessions yet. Pick a question set below to start.
mockInterview.deleteSessionConfirm: This mock interview session will be permanently deleted.
mockInterview.feedbackFailed: Failed to get feedback. You can still continue.
mockInterview.feedbackUnavailable: (Feedback unavailable — AI service was temporarily unavailable.)
mockInterview.summaryNotAvailable: Summary not available. Your answers are still saved.

q1Tips: End with why you're excited about this role
genericQuestions.q2: Why are you interested in this position?
q2Tips: Connect your skills to the role
genericQuestions.q3: Describe a time you faced a challenge at work or school. How did you handle it?
q4Tips: Back each with a brief example
genericQuestions.q5: Where do you see yourself in five years?
genericQuestions.q6: Tell me about a time you worked on a team.

jobSearch.title: Job Search
jobSearch.description: Find openings through networking, job boards, and direct outreach.

networking.title: Networking
networking.description: List people who can help and plan how to reach out.

jobBoards.title: Job Boards & Websites
jobBoards.description: Where to search online and how to get results from each site.

checklist.title: Job Search Checklist
checklist.description: Track your progress across all search methods.

networking.title: Build Your Network
networking.description: Most jobs are found through connections. List the people you will reach out to.
networking.callout: Include people who are not in your line of work too. You never know who has a valuable connection. Share your power statement when networking, and always ask for advice.
networking.professionalDesc: Former employers, co-workers, teachers, classmates, mentors

jobBoards.title: Job Boards & Websites
jobBoards.description: Post your resume on multiple sites and set up alerts so openings come to you.
jobBoards.callout: Use a variety of sites, set job alerts, and use keyword-rich search terms. If a company's name appears, apply directly through their website when possible.
jobBoards.scamWarning: Watch out for scams. Be wary of listings that promise quick income, require fees, or ask for your Social Security number or bank info before hiring.

linkedin.name: LinkedIn
linkedin.tip: Build your profile, set job alerts, use Easy Apply

indeed.name: Indeed
indeed.tip: Upload resume, set daily email alerts by keyword

glassdoor.name: Glassdoor
glassdoor.tip: Research company culture and salary ranges

handshake.name: Handshake
handshake.tip: Connect with employers recruiting from your school

googleJobs.name: Google Jobs
googleJobs.tip: Search 'jobs near me'. It pulls listings from multiple sites.

usajobs.name: USAJobs.gov
usajobs.tip: Federal job listings with structured applications

ziprecruiter.name: ZipRecruiter
ziprecruiter.tip: AI-matched job recommendations based on your resume

checklist.title: Job Search Checklist
checklist.description: Check off each item as you go so you don't miss any channels.
progressItems: I can access my resume through email, cloud, or flash drive
I have registered with and completed profiles on job websites
I have registered with staffing agencies
I am contacting at least 2 people in my network each week
I have contacted companies through cold calls or walk-ins
I have attended recruitments or interviews
I practice my Power Statement regularly
I have attended Job Fairs or Career Expos
I follow up on each of my applications and interviews

socialMedia.title: Social Media
socialMedia.description: Clean up your profiles before employers Google you.

audit.title: Online Presence Audit
audit.description: Review your social media profiles through an employer's eyes.

linkedin.name: LinkedIn
linkedin.tip: Build a complete profile, add a professional photo, and request recommendations from professors or supervisors.

twitter.name: X (Twitter)
twitter.tip: Follow companies you want to work for. Share industry articles. Keep your handle professional.

instagram.name: Instagram
instagram.tip: Set personal accounts to private. If public, make sure posts match your professional brand.

facebook.name: Facebook
facebook.tip: Review tagged photos and old posts. Adjust privacy settings so only friends see personal content.

audit.title: Online Presence Audit
audit.description: Review your social media through an employer's eyes. Most hiring managers check candidates' social profiles, and many have passed on a candidate based on what they found.
audit.callout: Before starting your job search, Google yourself and review every social media profile. Set all personal accounts to private. Everything public is fair game for employers.
includeItems: Content interesting and relevant to your industry
Up-to-date industry news and shared articles
Profile matching your application and resume
dontIncludeItems: Images/posts that contradict your professional brand
Profanity or references to alcohol/drug use
Negative comments about past employers or co-workers
audit.bgCheckDesc: Review your posts and pictures. Would you pass an employer's screening in these categories?
bgCheckItems: Drug use, excessive drinking, or partying content
Sexually explicit or profane images and comments
Aggressive or violent acts or assertions
audit.needsWorkWarning: You have areas that need attention. Review and clean up those posts before starting your job search.

landingTheJob.title: Landing the Job
landingTheJob.description: Do well at your new job with workplace tips and a monthly self-check.

workplaceSuccess.title: Workplace Success
workplaceSuccess.description: Tips for doing well in your first job.

selfEvaluation.title: Self-Evaluation
selfEvaluation.description: Track your performance during your first months on the job.

workplaceSuccess.title: Long-Term Workplace Success
workplaceSuccess.description: The habits that get you noticed and promoted. Here's what managers actually pay attention to.
workplaceSuccess.callout: These habits matter at every stage of your career. Review them before your first week, then revisit monthly to stay on track.

workHabits.title: Work Habits
items: Maintain a professional image — live your brand
Arrive on time and call in promptly when you'll be late or absent
Limit personal cell phone use to breaks and rest periods
Show up with a good attitude — it gets noticed
Do not participate in office gossip

performance.title: Performance of Duties
items: Understand what is expected of you
Meet performance goals and standards
Be a team player — contribute to team objectives
Be neat and organized in all your tasks
If you make a mistake, take responsibility and communicate

communication.title: Communication & Feedback
items: Maintain professional relationships with co-workers and supervisors
Ask for and learn from feedback and constructive criticism
Speak up when there is a problem — follow the chain of command
Learn to disagree respectfully — listen carefully, then share your perspective
Treat customers in a friendly yet professional manner
Be respectful — no swearing, bullying, or inappropriate comments

growth.title: Professional Growth
items: Be willing to change and grow with the company
Be self-motivated and seek ways to remain positive and productive
Assist with tasks outside of your job description when possible
Support and participate in company activities

personalLife.title: Personal Life Balance
items: Keep personal life out of the workplace — don't let challenges affect your performance
Use good judgment when sharing personal information with co-workers
Understand and follow company guidelines regarding social media
Do not drink alcohol or use drugs before or during work hours

selfEvaluation.title: New Employee Self-Evaluation
selfEvaluation.description: Track your progress at 1, 3, and 6 months. Rate yourself honestly on each item.
selfEvaluation.callout: Most jobs have a 3-6 month probation period. Use this to spot weak areas before your boss does.
selfEvaluation.ratingScale: 1 = Poor   2 = Fair   3 = Average   4 = Above Average   5 = Outstanding

workHabits.title: Work Habits
items: I understand and follow company rules and regulations
I show up on time and call in promptly when late or sick
I maintain good personal hygiene and dress appropriately
I limit personal cell phone use to breaks and rest periods
I am honest with time reporting and don't misuse company resources

performance.title: Performance of Duties
items: I understand my job duties and responsibilities
I am meeting performance goals and standards
I look for ways to use my skills to benefit the organization
I am confident in my ability to perform my work assignments

communication.title: Communication & Feedback
items: I relate to co-workers and customers professionally
I respond well to constructive criticism and learn from feedback
I speak up when there is a problem and admit mistakes
I treat co-workers with respect — no inappropriate behavior

growth.title: Professional Growth
items: I seek ways to remain productive without being told
I have learned new skills
I have assisted with tasks outside my job description
I add something my team would miss if I left

contactLog.title: Contact Log
contactLog.description: Track every application, follow-up, and employer interaction.
contactLog.emptyDesc: Add your first company to keep track of every application, follow-up, and offer.
contactLog.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

form.notesPlaceholder: How you found this job, contact person, etc.

error.title: Contact log error
error.description: Something went wrong loading your contact log. Your saved data is safe.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: This page doesn't exist. Check the URL or head back home.

global.title: Something went wrong
global.description: Something broke on our end, not yours. Your data is safe in your browser.

app.title: Something went wrong
app.description: Something broke on our end, not yours. Your saved data is safe in your browser.
